question: Does formal per-domain council increase expert utilization above 15%?
method: Count all DOMEX lanes from SWARM-LANES.md + SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md + git log. Compute DOMEX-session utilization (sessions with >=1 DOMEX lane / total sessions) per window. Cross-validate with gather_council.py for live seat status.

baseline.note: Original F-EXP3 baseline (L-376, S306): 75 long-lived lanes / 1628 slots = 4.6%. Methodology later corrected (L-889): per-session domain coverage 14.8% -> 10.8% (L-902). The 4.6% represents pre-council accumulated utilization.

windows.note: Incomplete window (2/10 sessions). Rate 100% but absolute count 2 < 3. Meets target by rate, not absolute count.

ten_session_windows_fscale2_metric.note: All complete 10-session windows meet >= 3 DOMEX sessions. Final window is incomplete (2 sessions, both DOMEX).

notes: Post-council DOMEX adoption was near-immediate: S335-S360 already 92.3%.
S391+ shows 100% utilization — every session runs at least one DOMEX lane.
Council health recovered from CRITICAL (2/10 at S408) to HEALTHY (9/10 at S426).
F-SCALE2 10-session window target (>=3 DOMEX sessions) met in all 9 complete windows. Range: 8-10 DOMEX sessions per window.
Domain diversity peaked at 40 domains (S335-S360), stabilized at 26-28 (S391-S420).

The 4.6% baseline used a different methodology (accumulated active lanes / capacity slots). Per-session coverage metric (L-889, L-902) gives 10.8% solo, 14.8% bundled. Both are below 15%, confirming the council mechanism is what pushed utilization above target.
Caveat: utilization measures session-DOMEX co-occurrence, not expert depth. F-EXP3 (RESOLVED) measured per-session domain coverage at 10.8% solo ceiling — the 15% target requires bundled sessions.
